Author: Steve Ralph
Comment:
Carnival in Venice is fantastic to see as a spectator, as we did this year in February.
You will witness ordinary people strolling the streets in extraordinary outfits.
These outfits are not cheap. The last price I heard was â¬500 a day as the norm.
That was several years ago.
Or you could be wearing the outfit and be famous for a day or more as hundreds take your picture.
There are also masked balls, dinners, afternoon tea, etc.

We did take part some years back, and it was a blast.

Author: shirley reliable
Comment:
Three year ago, I went to Italy for hoilday, and it is the first time to buy the doll from yours, nice people and lovely Triangel.        Last month, it is the 2nd time to buy the doll on line with a discount price.   Although it is discount price,  but the quality was the same and the dolls was so nice and I love it very much, and it is a very good Christmas gift for me.      I like the smile face for the Triangel the most, hope to see you again in Italy very soon.

Thanks again Grace for helping me to buy the wedding gift for friend!   It is so nice for a couple doll with the sofa.
I am also buying the birthday gift for me, the green cuddle triangle is so lovely with the smiling face!    Stay well and always have a good day for you!

Author: Cody P
Comment: We truly loved this shop! When we came to Venice we knew that we wanted some REAL Venetian masks, not the fake ones you see on the streets. I picked out a couple, some were gifts and the recipients loved them! They have a wide selection of designs and styles, and all are reasonably priced considering that they are hand made - and the quality is top-notch! I could have spent hours in this store, and I'd love to come back someday if I find myself in Venice again. Thank you for our favorite gifts from Italy!

Author: Yuxin Liu
Comment: Strongly recommended for someone who visit Venice and is interested in hand-made local souvenirs. Everything inside is amazingly beautiful. The owners are very patient and helpful. Will visit this shop again next time in Venice!!

Author: Hui Carmen
Comment: I had a wonderful shopping experience! The TriAngel is beautifully crafted with intricate details. The costume is stunning, and its facial expression is lively and warm. The seller was incredibly kind, even including a small Christmas gift for me. I wish the shop all the best and will definitely recommend it to my friends for purchasing TriAngels!

We all loved it and want to come back to explore the area more!

Author: Katie Weiler
Comment: Denise is an excellent tour guide! She is extremely knowledgeable, personable accommodating, and funny. I highly recommend her! We loved spending time with her!

Author: RM
Comment: Simone took our group to the Dolomites. He was one of the BEST guides we have ever had. Knowledgeable and entertaining he made our day a blast. He made us feel immersed in the Italian culture and history. Priceless.

Author: Trickyloki
Comment: Loredana was amazing and very informational. She went through the tour at a very easy pace without it feeling overwhelming amount of information.

Author: Felicia Green
Comment: From the train station, to the boat, taxi, to the downtown area Venice is BOMB! great stores, great food but in the tourist areas they are little pricey.  Ive only spent a day so far but trust and believe that I will be back!!

Author: Victoria Kellaway
Comment:
Amazing hotel. Staff are lovely and attentive. Rooms spotless and very comfortable. Beautiful bar. Would highly recommend you go and have a drink in the barâ¦. Especially if Massimo is there, heâs wonderful, he will make your evening. All the staff were 5 star. The breakfast selection is fantastic, itâs one of the best breakfast buffets weâve ever had in any hotel we have stayed in. The view of the canal is so pretty. Very easy to get to the tourist spots from the hotel either by walking or water bus, there is a stop just outside the door. If you want a stunning hotel that in a quite area but 2 minutes away from lovely restaurants and shops itâs perfect.
Thank you so much to all the staff, we will definitely return.
Victoria & David. ð®ð¹

Author: Daniel Balmer
Comment:
Perfect.

Me and my Mrs came for our honeymoon and it didn't disappoint.

From the moment when we arrived we felt welcome and at ease. I ordered some extras for the room, and these were delivered better than I expected.

The rooms were lovely. The canal view was spectacular. The breakfast was fantastic. And the bar was a perfect stop to start or end your night!

The location i personally thought was great. 15 mins away from the bus station. The walks to Venice from there was what made the holiday even better to discover the beautiful sites.

Thankyou so much!
We will return one day

Author: Brittany Napper
Comment: We loved our stay at the Carnival Palace. This trip to Venice was just for my husband and I and we loved the red room. Romantic, beautiful, clean, and quiet. The breakfast in the mornings was wonderful. I loved how close we were to the train station and the Cannaregio area is our favorite area to stay in in Venice.

Author: Nick Turney
Comment: Amazing hotel! The staff were just fantastic. Nothing was too much trouble. We came to Venice about 23 years ago and had always wanted to come back. Just amazing place. They gave us a few excellent recommendations for some local restaurants which were spot on. Although it was a good walk to the centre of Venice (hence the 4 stars, but there isn't anything they can do about that), it meant we had some amazing long walks. We got a water taxi from the airport and that was worth it. The best way to get to the hotel. Happily come back to stay here

Author: Nirali Bhatt
Comment: This hotel was absolutely beautiful. It was our first time in Italy and I am so glad our first stop was this hotel. The rooms are beautiful, the shower is amazing, beds are one of the most comfortable Iâve been in when traveling. On top of that the service is immaculate. The staff is so kind and friendly. I couldnât recommend this hotel enough. The breakfast included is cherry on top!

Author: Seokjin Ham
Comment: This is a special event held during the Venice Carnival, set within the historic Teseâformer warehouse buildingsâof the Arsenale, the cityâs traditional naval shipyard. This unique venue transforms into a vibrant stage for music, performances, and festivities, blending Veniceâs maritime heritage with its world-famous carnival spirit.

Comment: A true spectacle. The projections are amazing. We went for the story of Casanova. Canât wait for next year.

Author: Michael Offord
Comment:
A stunning theatre with a wonderful history.

The âPhoenixâ has risen a number of times, most recently after a fire in 1996.

In the tour, there is a touching tribute to the late, great Maria Callas, who performed here in many roles over many years.

The ball room and Apollo rooms are also very beautiful.

Author: Gaurav Gautam
Comment: Beautiful Opera House, just 8 min walk from St Marks Square. You can get a family ticket for (â¬30) along with an audio guide to enjoy the self guided tour. Ideal to be inside somewhere seeing this little gem on a hot day. We had a rehearsal going on too. We didn't need to pre book, just walked in and got the tickets. It was not very crowded.

Author: Bethel Selfcatering
Comment:
1. Atmosphere & Architecture

The theatre is absolutely gorgeous â gilded, ornate, with that period elegance. Golden decorations, frescoes, chandeliers, woodwork, plush red or roseâvelvet seating, all contribute to an immersive experience.

Thereâs a powerful sense of history: La Fenice has burned down and been restored multiple times (notably a major fire in 1996), yet each reconstruction has honored the original style. That gives the place layers of resilience, almost mythic quality, which heightens the emotional impact.

2. Acoustics & Performance

The sound is often praised â many reviews say that the acoustics are excellent; the theatre skillfully balances the orchestra, the singers, and the ambiance so that even from the boxes the intimacy isnât lost.

When there is a live performance (opera, symphony, ballet), everything seems to fall into place: the space, the architecture, the music and performance combine in a way that seems to transport people â many say they get chills.

3. Cultural Significance & Emotion

La Fenice has hosted many premieres by Verdi, Rossini, Donizetti, etc. Itâs a place where musical history was made. That gives a visit or performance an extra layer of weight and meaning.

The restoration after tragedies (fires), the passion to rebuild âcomâera, dovâeraâ (âas it was, where it wasâ) adds a sense of phoenixâlike rebirth. Itâs fitting symbolically.

4. Accessibility & Visitor Experience

Audio guides get good marks. They help bring the history, the architecture, stories of past performances alive for visitors.

The cost of a visit is often considered reasonable for what one gets (â¬15-â¬25
for Sanpaolo). Some familyâpricing options exist.

Author: Khalil Awad
Comment:
A breathtaking historic theater â worth the visit
Teatro La Fenice is one of the most beautiful opera houses Iâve seen. The interior is grand and elegant, full of history and charm. Even if youâre not attending a performance, the guided tour gives a fascinating glimpse into Veniceâs cultural past. The ticket price is a bit high for a short visit, but the experience and atmosphere make it worthwhile.

Comment:
When planning our holiday to Italy, the wife said one of the only things she definitely wants to do is a gondola ride.

Im super tight with money and when I saw the price, â¬90 for 30 minutes, I thought thats a rip off, I had to hope she'd forget about it.

We stumbled across Christians, honestly, it was getting close to 7pm when the price goes up and I was sweating paying â¬90 let alone â¬110.

He welcomed us and took us for a very quiet and peaceful gondola ride, pointing out key buildings,  answered questions we asked. The small quiet canel ended up popping on to the grand canel for abit, which was also quieting down too. After a while we then came back into the smaller canels and then ended up back at the beginning after so some tight corners.

I got off feeling that was actually worth â¬90. Definitely try and time a ride for around 6.20pm where the waterways are slowing down.

Thanks Christian keep up the good work.

Comment: Beautiful Opera House, just 8 min walk from St Marks Square. You can get a family ticket for (â¬30) along with an audio guide to enjoy the self guided tour. Ideal to be inside somewhere seeing this little gem on a hot day. We had a rehearsal going on too. We didn't need to pre book, just walked in and got the tickets. It was not very crowded.

Author: Bethel Selfcatering
Comment:
1. Atmosphere & Architecture

The theatre is absolutely gorgeous â gilded, ornate, with that period elegance. Golden decorations, frescoes, chandeliers, woodwork, plush red or roseâvelvet seating, all contribute to an immersive experience.

Thereâs a powerful sense of history: La Fenice has burned down and been restored multiple times (notably a major fire in 1996), yet each reconstruction has honored the original style. That gives the place layers of resilience, almost mythic quality, which heightens the emotional impact.

2. Acoustics & Performance

The sound is often praised â many reviews say that the acoustics are excellent; the theatre skillfully balances the orchestra, the singers, and the ambiance so that even from the boxes the intimacy isnât lost.

When there is a live performance (opera, symphony, ballet), everything seems to fall into place: the space, the architecture, the music and performance combine in a way that seems to transport people â many say they get chills.

3. Cultural Significance & Emotion

La Fenice has hosted many premieres by Verdi, Rossini, Donizetti, etc. Itâs a place where musical history was made. That gives a visit or performance an extra layer of weight and meaning.

The restoration after tragedies (fires), the passion to rebuild âcomâera, dovâeraâ (âas it was, where it wasâ) adds a sense of phoenixâlike rebirth. Itâs fitting symbolically.

4. Accessibility & Visitor Experience

Audio guides get good marks. They help bring the history, the architecture, stories of past performances alive for visitors.

The cost of a visit is often considered reasonable for what one gets (â¬15-â¬25
for Sanpaolo). Some familyâpricing options exist.

Author: sugapapa
Comment: This is my third time in Venice, but my first time at the Carnival. The Venice Carnival is counted as one of the three biggest carnivals in the world. It is said that the 18th century festival, where people hide their identities by wearing gorgeous costumes and masks, was revived as an event in the late 20th century and continues to this day. There are about 3 million participants in total, and the city is crowded with tourists, with costumed gentlemen and ladies walking around St. Mark's Square in particular. Whether you go into the back alleys or out in the square, you will find groups and couples in elaborate costumes who will pose for you when you point your camera at them. Just looking at the costumes and masks is fun, and I enjoyed the carnival in the midst of the bustle, taking lots of photos. Although such commotion is not bad once in a while, it is wise to avoid the carnival season if you want to take your time sightseeing around Venice's sights.

Author: Kashvili
Comment: "It's February and Venice is alive with Carnevale. There are high spots like the Balls, the Grand opening and closing ceremonies - but I went with my camera to capture the spirit. People appear in full costume with/without masks as you wander through the streets and canals. Others capture this on cameras. There are photo shoots- very early in the morning - and it seems to be by word of mouth where other shoots take place. Most times it was fun but - amateurs pretending to be paparazzi saw a few scuffles break out!! "Before breakfast "I thought!! A lot of action takes place in St Mark's Square at night - the collonades are lit with cascades of light, there is a centre stage and everything is alive. It's very surreal, fantastic and I am.very glad I have experienced it. Maybe one year I shall take a costume, buy a mask and wander the streets to be photographed and adored!"

It was an incredible experience, would highly recommend it. I would recommend to get there a bit earlier, we arrived very late and it was a challenge to fond a spot to see the boats. For the morning parade we arrived earlier and it was perfect!

Author: natashacastro

The dresses, atmosphere, decor, food and beverage presentations , attractions and shows ... everything makes you have a unique experience filled with intense great sensations !!! Total bucket list to experience hunters ;-)

Author: Clare S
Comment: I visited during the week and it was busy but not wild busy as on a weekend. The atmosphere is always good whether sunshine or rain. People go to dress up in all variety of costume and it's best to expect the unexpected. I did both dressing up in costume I took with me to save money and people watching. The theme this year was Circus. The more quirky the costume or the bigger the costume grabs the attention of everyone else. There are people who wear masks with everyday clothes to traditional costumes to anything else you can think of. It's anytime of day or night people are dressed up and you simply can't see everyone. If you have any preconceptions of what people will be or should be wearing - that will be changed. I saw superhero's, birds, steampunk, traditional costumes, kimono's, historical figures - anything and everything. The big savings are deals on hotels, (I don't know if all 5 star hotels do this but they have a 'policy' of taking 50 euros a day out of your current account for hotel purchases. Where I stayed they classed it as nothing - not much money. So if you do stay at a five star hotel be prepared for them to ask for money up front for in house purchases despite the stay being paid for as they may not let you into the hotel room otherwise.) and I make my own outfit to take with me and was £130. The amount of times I wear it the cheaper it is so for me say two occasions works out at £65 a wear. I went to the theatre and party galleon cruise. The best dressed competition is free and wore my dress then, plus the party galleon cruise. Other than that didn't have any more pennies for other expensive events. The ateliers charge 300 euros to hire an outfit so factor that in when booking an event - so say 250 euro for an event plus your outfit hire of 300 euros for each event if not making an outfit makes it more expensive. The best time to go is more during the week and avoid the weekends as it is uber busy, plus more expensive in hotels. The more people that do dress up the better it is for everyone for the atmosphere. The stage in St Mark's Square was playing music on a night and had the lights going to. If dressing up or seeing a quirky costume doesn't make you smile and make you happy well. I purchased a venizia unica card and tailor made it to include La fenice tour (10 euros), St Marks Sq museums, alilaguna ferry to airport, waterbus pass for 24 hours. It's good value to tailor make your own card to your own needs. If you need to get a water taxi only do so if there is a real need as they are expensive. The waterbus pass is ideal and good for sightseeing and resting your tired feet too. If you wish to have your photo taken in costume you can hire from an atelier and I think it went up to 90 euros. There are other companies but are more expensive too. I just did selfies or got someone to take my photo while in costume. Watch out for guys handing out 'free' roses please don't take them as they are not free and some people that pose with you in costume but want money or they try take your camera off you. Most don't do that including myself who was in costume but someone tried to do that to me so it's just to avoid any problems. I do love and enjoy Venice but felt this time my attitude towards money and Venice's attitude towards money are two different things. There is a level of money Venice is used to and comes to expect it a bit to easy I would say. I saved for three years, made my dress to go etc. A lot of work has gone into my holiday for my custom not to be valued maybe taken for granted. I would just love a happy medium. You can go through uber lots of money to come to Venice but this is if you have plenty. Just think of your budget and don't look through rose tinted glasses so much. If you have lots of money they fuss over you and it's seen in the expensive events. It is lovely to go and see a different way of living. I always think of staying near where an event or ball is within easy walking distance to avoid water taxis or getting lost in the labyrinth of streets late at night.

Author: Alessandro-Pietro
Comment: I was a bit sceptical about travelling to Venice during the carnival. After only one minute at San Marco I changed my mind. The masks and costumes were amazing as well as mysterious. The City of Venice is the best spot for the masked people to put in scene. They fit in perfectly in the mediaval piazzas and stimulate a lot the imagination. Masked people were not exclusively venetians but french, americans, british and germans as well. They delighted all the enthusiastic spectators, who took a lot of pictures and created a special atmosphere in a very special city.

Author: AndrissaFerreira
Comment: Without knowing it, we found ourselves in Venice for the launch of Carnivale! Locals and tourists alike don fabulous masks to walk the streets and there are many costumed revelers everywhere. Many of the artisanal mask makers can be seen finishing their lovely items deep into the night. An old tradition which will hopefully never cease. The Grand Canal is taken over by numerous rowboats decorated in a variety of themes. From a giant rat to a tango gondola - music, colour and the best of Venetian tradition.

Author: Julien K
Comment: Went the second time this year to the carnival of Venice. Last time, visited La Festa della Marie, which was really great. This time went on the first weekend of the carnival for La Festa Veneziana sull acqua. This is the more traditional event for the locals and happens mainly along the Cannaregio Canal. Be there at least 1.5 hours before it starts, because people will line up and you see it best if you are in the front row. First part is in the evening with boats, costumes & light, second part before lunch with a big boat parade on the Canal Grande, ending in Cannaregio Canal.

Author: ATomas
Comment: That was an experience for an life time . I have visited Venice on first week of Feb and on the first day it was raining badly .Still it didn't spoil the fun .I will say it was the best experience in all venice trip :)

Author: Mikey1214
Comment: We spent a week at the Carnival which was excellent. Daily entertainment on the large stage from Dancing, musicians, circus performers, twice daily costume/mask parades and music events in the evening all helped with the happy atmosphere. Many people were in costume more wearing masks. We took our own costumes as hiring can be quite expensive. We were told we would probably be stopped ,but did not realise the scale of people wanting to have photographs taken with us. We were happily surprised. We didn't go to any of the Carnival Balls and Dinners as, again you needed a larger money budget. We had looked into attending and some of the venues, also, were out of the main area of Venice and some of the comments regarding the food weren't good. The final day of the Carnival was to a packed St. Mark's Square with the flying of the huge flag from the stage area up to the top of the tower. If you get the chance do go and if possible dress up, you will enjoy the experience even more.

Author: vitu16
Comment: This is my second time in Venice. I think Venice is a tourist trap place. But when they have carnival ... its cool! Especially if you like to take photos, portrait or even street. You will get lot of shots there. Things that need to know is need to see calendar and events, date first and manage yr time, advance booking for long will help a lots.

Author: Rider123
Comment: Trying to get to most important world carnivals, Venice was a must and didn't disappoint. This celebration is all about stand and model, why not in the country that invented it. Walk the streets and snap the photos and take in the wonder that is Venice.

Author: Dee L
Comment: A magnificent, colourful, pageant of surreal characters, brightening the square and alleyways of Venice with their eclectic mix of masks and costume; at one moment chatting, in the next posing as if they had been born a la mode; a wildly, colourful collection of sights that you never get tired of photographing. If you can dress up, do so, even in a mask and cloak, you feel enveloped in the whole adventure of it all. I loved every moment in this beautiful city and will definitely go back in costume one day soon. Something to be experienced and not missed.

Author: PavlaPavla
Comment: Carnival of Venice it's definitely worth visiting. Of course, there are thousands and thousands tourists, almost every place in the town is full of people, but there is a very special atmosphere too...and many breathtaking masks everywhere. When I arrived in Venice, it was rainy early morning. Almost no one was on St. Marcus Square and in near area. Yes, because it was raining, there were not almost any masks in that time too. :-) But it changed in few hours, when the rain stopped. Everywhere appeared people dressed in beautiful masks. I felt like in a fairy tale. Sometimes it was really difficult to take a picture of a mask, because everybody wanted to take pictures of those amazing costumes. There was a parade too. Approximately 100 people dressed in costumes went to the St. Marcus Square. There were drummers, people with flags, beautiful ladies in blue dresses. There was the drummers show too. You could see everything on a huge screen. It was the best time for taking pictures of masks during this parade, because a lot of people watched it and it was not so crowded around the masks.

Author: Ron A
Comment: It is a big disappointment to find that the city of Venice, which is based on truism, cannot be able to organize a normal access to the main carnival opening event that took place on the night of January 27, 2018. Most of the people that came to Venice especially for this event, found themselves struggling to find a way to the Rio di Cannaregio, pushing between thousands of people either trying to reach the event or leaving the scene area, without being able to see anything. So, if you have some thoughts visiting this kind of event, think again. ? Especially if you have kids! it was too crowded and risky. The policemen stood helpless, smoking cigarettes instead of helping mothers with children or adults that almost couldn't breathe. In conclusion, it was a traumatic event and after almost 4 hours we didn't even reach any point along the canal (less than 1 km).

Author: KingFrog1
Comment: This is the first parade show of Carnival in Venice....We went 1 hour before the show. It was so crowded people were turned away. Police had a hard time holding the lines back. There were so many people, crowds were at a near standstill on Lista di Spagna. We thought about waiting for the 2nd show at 8pm but found out that people were directed to go to the train station to get in through 3 access ways there. The crowd was backed up at least 0.1 mile (past Hotel Principe) trying to get in to a small segment of water way. The street was filled with people heading to the train station, with only a single file line of people heading in the opposite direction. There was too much media and not enough room, and not enough organization to indicate access locations. We realized we were not going to make the 2nd show and this was at 6:20 2pm. Too many people, not enough show... strike that...no show. Attached are pics of the crowd and a quick shot over the blockade fence on the bridge where the police made everyone move along....I dread tomorrows part 2 show....

Author: Sara D
Comment: When we arrived at this stunning place, little did we know this city would win our hearts in no time. The train station was crowded with people from all over the world, but we easily got to the centre of the city and, most essentially, to the main location of the event. After purchasing the citys map at the train station, it took us about half an hour to get to St. Marcus Square. While heading to our destination, we were simply overwhelmed by the ancient monuments and the festive atmosphere the Venetians created to make the carnival unforgettable. Having reached the square, our attention was captured by a high-wire artist who was flamboyantly dressed. When the music started, he flew above our heads and crossed the whole square; this performance is known as: Il volo dellangelo (The flight of the angel). We were amazed by the show and couldnt take our eyes off the elaborated masks surrounding us. People wore them in different sizes and colours and transmitted the feeling of being thrown back in time. The dressed-up men and women danced and acted across the city with their outstanding costumes. Even if Venice was crowded with tourists and all kinds of languages were spoken, we still could live our own fairy-tale thanks to its surreal atmosphere enriched by Italians and their culture.

Author: Stefano F
Comment: Went with my girlfriend and it was a magical experience! It is cultural and romantic. I suggest booking parties to attend like one on a boat and/or hotel events so that you may dress up and experience amazing food and performances! Buy your own handmade masks from the authentic shops! An experience you must do at least once in your life!

Author: bluegwladys
Comment: Venice is a magical city at the best of times, but if you can visit during Carnevale, then make sure you go! Many people choose to take part in the daily costume competition in St Mark's Square but you can still soak up the atmosphere around the city without being part of this, turning a corner and encountering people in full regalia. Events are ongoing over two weeks so it's worth checking what captures your interest the most and planning your trip to include it. The opening ceremony on the Cannaregio canal is a real spectacle and the ballad of the masks, a parade through the streets from Dorsoduro to San Marco is great entertainment too!

Author: Clare S
Comment: I went to the carnival in 2015 and will be returning next year. I am doing this on a budget and also as an amateur photographer. I make my own costume as it's cheaper than hiring and will be going to a couple of events (the cheaper ones) [truncated]

Author: Vale S
Comment: There is no need to join parties or events, the entire Venice celebrates the Carnavale. Wear a costume (no need to spend a fortune renting one), go out and enjoy! For photography lovers, it's the best time to go to Venice, but beware, it's very crowded [truncated]

Author: MissRollaboard
Comment: The historical costumes--and fantastical variations on them--are my favorite. It's entertaining to see costumed people in the streets, but I suspect the private balls are another order of being [truncated]

Author: mendi62
Comment: It is not a carnival of big parades of groups or schools but a cheerful carnival of masks and costumes showed by singles, couples and small teams [truncated]

Author: daswitzer2017
Comment: My mom and I visited Venice for two nights during Carnival and it was the highlight of our Italy trip! Venice itself was wonderful, but the costumes, masks and lively atmosphere of Carnival made it magical. We traveled with only carryons so didn't have full costumes, however, we paired home-made black satin capes with gorgeous feather masks and joined in the fun. The (free) costume action is centred at St Mark's square where you can find elaborately costumed Carnival-goers happy to pose for pictures at all hours. I got the best photos early in the morning (think 7-8am) before the crowds built up. We had a wonderful time, and are already thinking about going back for another Carnival! Be aware that Carnival attracts lots of visitors, so be sure to book accommodation in advance and be prepared for high season prices.

Author: lcharette2016
Comment: We just spent 3 days in Venice for the Carnival and absolutely loved it. Many people walk around costumed or masked. Some of the costumes are very elaborate. We had brought costumes and wigs from home and bought our masks in Venice. We went to dinner fully costumed. Was so much fun! If you are to go during Carnival, do make a point of finding/renting a costume. You become part of the entertainment. If you can, avoid Carnival Sundays. All other days are fine, but Sundays during Carnival, Venice gets packed with people who come over only for the day: it gets difficult to walk around the main squares and attractions. The other days are not an issue at all. We went to a ball - Casanova at the Luna hotel. For the price you pay, you would expect great food, wine and entertainment. They were ok at best. The dancing however was a lot of fun. We found it difficult to find reviews on the various Venice balls. The few reviews we read pretty much all said the same : food and wine aren't great, but dancing is fun. Next time, we will simply purchase tickets for dancing at one of the balls.

Author: AL12345678
Comment: The Carnival of Venice is a unique opportunity to see people wearing elaborate costumes and masks, some of which are really fancy and elaborate. Nevertheless, if it is your first visit to Venice the carnival season may not be the best option due to the huge crowds it attracts and to the inability to explore the city at leisure. Moreover, apart from the fact that there are many masked people wearing intricate costumes, the events organised by the city are really badly organised - for example, for the Feste delle Marie, the procession not only did not depart on time, but many of the participants in the procession were left behind at the starting point and later, when they realised what happened, the organisers sent a guy who began shouting to those people to come along! For the transportation, during the peak weekend (18-19 Feb) it was impossible to board the vaporettos from the station due to overcrowding, although there were additional boats running. I suppose that for tickets that cost 7.50 euros one way one could at least expect to be able to board in a reasonable time. In spite these organisational limitations, the Carnival in Venice is a great spectacle!

Author: Angela B
Comment: To visit Venice anytime is an experience of a lifetime. If you visit during carnival it makes it even more special. Just moving around the city you see costume and mask clad revellers. They have slightly spooky look but are fascinating to watch. They will pose for you to take photographs at every opportunity. They are especially around the St Mark's square area. Confetti and streamers on the floor everywhere add to this beautiful city's charm. A must see city and a must see event.

Author: Club0-14
Comment: We 'accidentally travelled to Venice during Carnevale and on arriving at our hotel and dumping our bags went out for a walk. In about a mile we saw mimes, jugglers, fire-eaters, acrobats and costumed actors. Unbelievably intricate costumes and masks are worn by people from all over the world with characters looking like medieval royalty mingling with the cast of Mad Max.

Author: Jill M
Comment: People wear the most wonderful costumes and masks in the streets, posing for photos. The whole carnival atmosphere is just lovely. I'm so glad we were in Venice then.

Author: jacklynnewine
Comment: An unmissable Carnival of street theatre, masks, costumes, just unforgettable! Sweet treat of Fratelli Venizia (similar to doughnut) irresistible!

Author: SaltPepperAndSpice
Comment: We spent the day in Venice on Tuesday, travelling by train from Verona. We bought a days ticket for the water bus and buzzed up and down the Grand Canal walking through to the Lagoon side and then caught the vaporetta to St Marks Square where we wandered about watching the various masked and costumed couples who posed happily for photos. Our two teenagers and 10 year old all wanted masks and were intrigued by the spectacle and it was spectacular. We were there during the day and the crowds were actually sparse. It was a great day.

Author: KevLes41
Comment: Weather is a little cooler but no cruise hoards! Every corner you walk around there are new and spectacular costumes - take part as well and wander the streets in your own mask or costume - great fun and lovely atmosphere.

Author: ViaVolta
Comment: We have stayed 2 nights in Venice. Beautiful, until this morning. Opening of the carnival. I don't know where the people came from. It was nice and quiet the past days but this morning loads of people. All with selfie sticks... The costumes are very nice so are the masks. But it is getting a bit "cheap". With cheap material costumes and plastic masks. It would be nice if they could keep the authentic tradition with nice costumes. The weather was beautiful, sunny and warm. I enjoyed the parade of the costumes.

Author: Christelle E
Comment: The Venice Carnival opening 2017 featured a show on the water, where inflatable objects and people representing Life and Nature paraded through the Canale.

Author: ITRT
Comment: During our walking tour, I wondered why there were so many shops selling masks. Our guide said masks were part of a centuries-old tradition of Venice. The masks are usually worn during the Carnival of Venice. The masks allowed the lower and upper classes to mingle. Early masks were plain while most of the masks today feature bright colors and the use of Baroque-style decorations. We were not there during Carnivale but I understand it is quite a celebration.

Author: casperia
Comment: My first visit to Carnevale was in 2012 with my husband, we were in awe and had an incredible time. I returned the next 2 years with friends and my teen daughter, and now I lead Carnevale tours for small groups. It's so much fun, I love the amazing costumes. It's like a huge masquerade street party, everyone is so elegant. Yes it gets insanely crowded on the weekends, but put on your costume and get into it! Attend a masked ball, go out for drinks in costume to one of the old cafes on St Marks square, glide on the canal in a gondola... create your own costume parade through St Marks square and feel like a celebrity posing for photos behind your mysterious mask. It's the grandest party you can imagine, and something you should do at least once in your life. Go have some fun in this magical ancient lagoon city. I highly recommend it! Just try to be respectful of the Venetians, this isn't a Mardi Gras drunk fest, and if you dress up... please dress elegant in a themed Renaissance period costume, this isn't Halloween. Last tip, do dress up. Life is so much more fun when you participate!

Author: Deee_Naa
Comment: Irrespective of the fact that Venezia is crowded during the carnival period, it's a must visit if you can make it. The glamorous carnival costumes ranging from children to elders and even for cute lil puppies, catches your eye and will be remembered for a lifetime!

Author: John F
Comment: Despite the crowds at the big events like The Flight of the Angel and at weekends this is an amazing event. If you can, be there for a few midweek days. It is worth it. The crowds are less but there are still lots of people in costume to check out. For the full experience go in costume for a few hours. All those times you wanted to be on display and were too embarrassed - now you can flaunt your inner extrovert with ease. Even more so with a mask. If you are feeling rich there are dozens of balls and the like to attend - but they do not come cheap: 300 euros is the starting price.

Author: PaulClaydon
Comment: I dare anyone to visit Venice and not fall in love with this most wonderful city. We stage managed our second visit to coincide with the last two days of this amazing carnival. They say that Venice is selling its soul to tourism, but it has adapted itself to survive I think. I cannot speak for other years, but an elaborate ornate series of exhibits are erected in St.Marks square with a stage facing the basilica. Confetti was thrown freely and everyone seemed to be dragged into the spirit of the carnival. There were a vast array of costumes on display from the novelty, to the home made and best of all the traditional stunning costumes. There were fancy dress competitions, live musicians and all sorts of fun to be had. The locals are obviously proud of their city and carnival tradition as so many dress up and play their role. They are quite happy to pose for photographs if they are asked politely. Everywhere you go masked costumed people are walking around. To be honest it is a little surreal (I best describe it as being in a Doctor Who episode). It is hard to be unmoved by the spectacle and found yourself becoming involved whether you wanted to or not. Long may it continue.

Author: Sandra B
Comment: It was second time we returned for Venice carnival this year. It is something one must see. I didn't wear the mask because when one becomes mask, it is not possible to experience anything else, the game is on. One of the grandest and most sincere celebrations on the planet, refilling centuries old Serenissima with life and mystery every year. Be there. Be a mask, or an observer, or photo-hunter, be a part of Venice Carnival.

Author: CMorgan
Comment: The Carnival of Venice is probably the most famous carnival in the world (along with the one in Rio) and it still brings the old times' atmosphere around the city during the cold months. It's a nice opportunity to wear a famous mask on the streets and to see many extraordinary costumes.

Author: Sandra B
Comment: This was my second time visiting the carnival and my third time in Venice. I simply adore the city. It is so beautiful and interesting, especially in the time of the carnival. There are so many people in masks and costumes walking all around the city making the atmosphere of the city even more romantic. It is for sure a day well spent. Weather in Venice in February is quite warm, the only negative thing in the time of carnival is that there is a lot of people, so walking across the bridges in some parts of the city can be quite a challenge and a bit time consuming. I will definitely go back in a few years.

Author: Colin_Frendo
Comment: Not really a fan of Carnival but took my wife on a surprise trip to Venice for Carnival 2016 (which was high on her bucket list) and ended up enjoying it too. The large array of colourful and diverse costumes worn by locals and tourists alike are a sight to behold. Many a photo opportunity set against the scenic back drop of the Venetian lagoon and Piazza S. Marco. Most costume wearers are happy to oblige. Some carnival activity is present everyday in the piazza and there was also a small fireworks show at the Arsenale di Venezia.

Author: Jan G
Comment: Amazing costumes. So many people dressed up from all over the world. Plenty of places to buy masks yourself from 2 upwards. Go into the shops in the back streets for bargains. Great atmosphere.

Author: Jeroen W
Comment: The Carnival of Venice is a fun and beautiful experience of its history, tailored artistry, a very special atmosphere and a conglomeration of many interesting personalities. This carnival is not a bacchanal, it is a mysterious Carnivalesque self expression by the participants with a veil of anonymity. All participants wear masks, some of which are truly fine works of art and most wear costumes to match. The variety and creativeness of the characters is astounding as is the exemplary patience of the Venetians (and many foreign) participants with the tourists who sometimes almost ambush them with their smart phones. The detail of some costumes is crazy, we were in a restaurant with two couples that had Renaissance costumes, including the hooped skirts and very uncomfortable girdles for the ladies and wigs for all four. At the end of their dinner we were asked to help them put on the mask, for it was nearly impossible for them to do this themselves. Committed! This is an experience of a lifetime if you can take the time to appreciate it.

Author: ceejaydee
Comment: After ten years and superb memories, I went to the carnival! The crowds of Chinese tourists bothering the costumed characters, the overnumerous 'selfies' and the angry photographers, the vulgar Carnival trinkets sold by Tamil vendors, the beggars, the African street vendors of bags. Crowds in narrow streets, the Canale border disfigured by ramps to get to the boat area, the gondolas at 90 for 20 min in blocked canals. Is Venice losing its soul before sinking with climate change? The costumes are not what they used to be!!!! A lot of amateurs with homemade disguises in curtain material! They do have fun, I reckon!! Good for them.

Author: Reg M
Comment: First trip to Venice and although it was a little busy it was fun, I guess it's to be expected as one of the most popular tourist destinations. Visiting at carnival time made the trip vibrant and colourful. It was great to see so many people dressed up in period costumes but also modern comical outfits that made us smile and laugh. People that weren't dressed up had masks so we're still participating in the spirit of carnival. Revellers continued late into the night around the streets in street parties and bars across the city. It was fun watching spectacles of people promenading, dancing, singing and generally having fun with friends and family. Lots of street artists and bands entertaining. There was a visible presence of The Carabinieri on the streets but I didn't see a single sign of trouble on any of the night outs we drink, ate and moved from bar to bar. In fact, we found two Carabinieri in a bar on their break, still tooled up, only in Italy. Haha! So yea, Carnival time is real fun, lots to see and do, go have a blast.

Author: nicolewsd
Comment: So before booking this I scoured the internet trying to find reviews on the balls without much success. I ended up going with Venice Events as all the reviews seemed really good. Getting there: It's easy enough to find and just a quick walk from the water bus stop. Entering: There were people greeting you along the way. The main room was everything you could want it to be. The entire room was just decadent. Food: The food was on point and the massive lobster tail was fantastic. After Dinner: You head back downstairs to the room you started in. Entertainment: The performers moved around the room so everyone got a chance to see everything. Costume: I knew I wanted to get really dressed up in one of the traditional costumes. I went to 2 locations and although we may not have looked traditional our costumes totally made up for it. Over the top is NOT in their vocabulary and thank god it wasn't. I hope this helps give you a bit more information.

Author: MrsRRD
Comment: A wonderful time to visit Venice, book early to avoid high hotel prices. The costumes are incredible and the city takes on a wonderful charm. Be warned though, it is so busy. We were in traffic jams of people trying to walk down some of the narrow streets with police directing people! The queues for all the attractions were ridiculously long. I would recommend going during the week to see attractions and the weekends for costumes!

Author: cgood511
Comment: While the carnival takes place in February it does not seem the best time to see Venice, but the costumes and atmosphere simply excites everyone. The Venetians put so much effort into their magnificent costumes, it is a photographer's paradise. We stayed just across the lagoon from Venice, which did involve some travelling each day, if the pocket allows, stay in Venice. You can get full enjoyment from all the concerts and the full programme of evening events. I would certainly visit again another year, there is still so much I would like to see, getting around Venice is so easy by public transport, also visiting some of the other islands in the lagoon can be done by water taxis.

Author: Anastasia S
Comment: We flew to Venice especially for the carnival and even had studied the program in advance and chosen some event we wanted to visit, but ended up doing our own thing. Even on "usual" days when no major events were planned, San Marco square and nearby areas were packed with tourists. So, getting around took loads of time and involved constantly being on alert not to become a victim of pickpockets. Crowds gathered around every person in a beautiful costume, which made taking pictures very challenging. I wonder what was going on there during major events. Since we also wanted to do some sightseeing, we skipped those events. All in all, if you have already seen Venice and are really interested in enjoying the carnival, you can either prepare psychologically and go spend several days in this crowd photo hunting, or put on an elaborated costume and become a local star with thousands of tourists taking pictures of you and uploading them in social media :)

Author: kralkriszti
Comment: It was my first time on Carnival and it is an unforgettable experience. The St Mark Square is amazing and local people are very friendly.

Author: Peter G
Comment: Not really a fan of Carnival but took my wife on a surprise trip to Venice for Carnival 2016 (which was high on her bucket list) and ended up enjoying it too. The large array of colourful and diverse costumes worn by locals and tourists alike are a sight to behold. Many a photo opportunity set against the scenic back drop of the Venetian lagoon and Piazza S. Marco. Most costume wearers are happy to oblige. Some carnival activity is present every day in the piazza and there was also a small fireworks show at the Arsenale di Venezia.

Author: AAA
Comment: All Venice just full of joy!!! People are excited, dressed up, there is music everywhere, everything is just beautiful and colorful. Just a shame it's cold at this time of year, but warmed my heart! Fun fun fun!!!

Author: thewanderer2016
Comment: Well, people dress up to varying degrees and there are shops and stalls devoted to costumes and accessories. For the few who go the whole hog, it is another reason to take photos with that sore camera finger! Was funny seeing the folks in the gallery of St Marks! In reality there are bigger and more flamboyant carnivals. Who picks up all that confetti?!

Author: Claire Y
Comment: It was just as amazing as all the photos show. The place is buzzing and very busy but there is so much going on all over the place! We saw the opening ceremony which was pretty awesome and the next day saw the pianist hanging over the Grand Canal playing a grand piano! Followed by a rendition of opera which resonated the length of the canal. Absolutely wonderful.

Author: _Broadbent
Comment: Although a little chilly, really glad we caught the beginning of the Carnivale. A wicked weekend was had, so much fun seeing the streets lined with masks and beautiful costumes. We had some amazing food and a glass of prosecco or 2. Wonderfully romantic.

Author: Simona 
Comment: We were on a one day trip there on the main day of the carnival. The masks are beautiful. We watched the drop of the angel from the tower and then walked through the side streets and just got lost in the beauty of Venice. The food and drinks are very expensive.

Author: Brad ????
Comment: Locals and those who regularly visit Venice may not enjoy being around during this time of the year but we have much enjoyed visits during the Carnival holiday period held yearly. What we enjoy most about visits during this time of the year is the festive environment. There is always an outdoor stage with evening performances at St. Mark's Square. Other smaller squares around Venice host events and small markets during Carnival as well. Throughout Venice you will also see participants dressed in lovely traditional Carnival costumes and masks, posing for you to take pictures. Hotels are very pricey during Carnival so try to reserve your hotel rooms well in advance. Trains departing Venice are quite full during Carnival, so reserve rail tickets in advance. If you want to buy Venetian masks, cheap souvenir quality masks are found at kiosks near popular tourist spots or in the endless souvenir shops. Higher quality masks are available at shops like Atelier Marega. A dedicated Carnival of Venice website provides general information and event details.

Author: anup s
Comment: What a day we chose to visit St Mark's square. It was the carnival month and it sure was a sight to see with thousands gathered early in the morning and dressed to the occasion. This is definitely a must visit in Venice.
